text: The patient is under general anesthesia in the lithotomy position. A long star retractor assists in the procedure. The prolapsed rectum is fully everted using Alice clamps. A circumferential electrocautery incision is made approximately one to two centimeters above the dentate line.

The surgeon sections the rectum circumferentially using a ligature blunt tip until reaching the peritoneum of the pouch of Douglas. The ventral side of the prolapsed bowel is visible as the peritoneum is opened.

The elongated bowel is drawn out through the opening in the peritoneum. The surgeon sections the mesocolon using a ligature blunt tip at the chosen site for colic section. The levator ani muscles are identified, and a posterior levatoroplasty is performed using interrupted absorbable sutures to enhance postoperative continence.

The surgeon references the anal canal in a cardinal manner and excises the prolapsed bowel progressively using electrocautery. Following the excision, a hand-sewn coloanal anastomosis is performed by suturing the proximal margin to the anal canal with interrupted 3-0 absorbable sutures, completing the anastomosis with additional interrupted sutures.
